docs.pageSubtitle: Everything you need to know about astro-intl

hero.title: Get Started with
hero.description: A <bold>type-safe</bold> internationalization library for Astro, inspired by next-intl. No props drilling, dynamic loading, and full TypeScript support.

installation.title: Installation
installation.description: Install astro-intl using your preferred package manager.
installation.addIntegration: Optionally register the integration in your astro.config.mjs:
installation.integrationNote: The integration is optional — it only logs a startup message. The core API works without it.

fileStructure.title: File Structure
fileStructure.description: astro-intl expects a specific folder structure inside your project. Here is the recommended layout:
fileStructure.pagesNote: Pages must be nested under a [lang] dynamic segment so Astro can generate one route per locale.
fileStructure.messagesNote: Each locale gets its own JSON file inside src/i18n/messages/. The filename must match the locale code exactly (e.g. en.json, es.json).
fileStructure.requestNote: The request.ts file exports a function that receives the locale string and returns the messages for that locale.

configuration.title: Configuration
configuration.requestDescription: This file is the bridge between the URL locale and your JSON messages. It receives the locale extracted from the URL and returns a RequestConfig object.
configuration.messagesDescription: Create one JSON file per locale inside src/i18n/messages/. You can organize keys into namespaces (nested objects).
configuration.layoutDescription: Best practice: Call setRequestLocale once in a shared layout (e.g., src/layouts/Layout.astro) instead of repeating it on every page. This initializes the global i18n context before any component reads translations.
configuration.staticPathsDescription: In every [lang] page, export getStaticPaths returning all supported locales so Astro can pre-render them.

usage.title: Usage
usage.basicDescription: Once your layout is configured, simply use getTranslations(namespace) in any page to get a typed translation function for a specific namespace.
usage.markupDescription: Use t.markup(key, tags) to interpolate HTML tags inside a translation string. The tags object maps tag names to functions that receive the inner content.
usage.reactDescription: Use getTranslationsReact(namespace) inside React components, then call t.rich(key, tags) to interpolate React nodes.

setRequestLocale.name: setRequestLocale
setRequestLocale.description: Extracts the locale from the URL pathname (first segment) and calls your getConfig function to load messages. Must be awaited before any other call.

getLocale.name: getLocale
getLocale.description: Returns the current locale string set by setRequestLocale. Throws if called before setting the locale.

getTranslations.name: getTranslations
getTranslations.description: Returns a typed t(key) function scoped to the given namespace. Also exposes t.markup(key, tags) for HTML interpolation.

getTranslationsReact.name: getTranslationsReact
getTranslationsReact.description: Same as getTranslations but returns a function with t.rich(key, tags) for React node interpolation.

requestConfig.name: RequestConfig
requestConfig.description: Type returned by your getRequestConfig function. Contains locale: string and messages: Record.

dotPaths.name: DotPaths<T>
dotPaths.description: Utility type that generates all valid dot-notation paths for a given messages object. Used internally to provide autocomplete for translation keys.

examples.title: Examples
examples.namespaceDescription: Namespaces let you split large translation files into logical sections. Pass the namespace name to getTranslations.
examples.typeDescription: Pass your messages type as a generic to getTranslations to get full autocomplete and type checking on translation keys.
examples.localeDescription: Use getLocale() anywhere after setRequestLocale has been called — for example to build locale-aware URLs.
